career_angle: The feature set is solid, but wait for third-party benchmarks or cost analysis before switching platforms—corporate blog launches always cherry-pick metrics in their favor.

dimensions.name: Source Authority
dimensions.rationale: Modal is a legitimate cloud compute company with real infrastructure and proven track record delivering GPU services. Credible messenger for compute topics.

dimensions.name: Evidence Quality
dimensions.rationale: Provides vanity metrics (5,000 accounts, 200,000 cells) and testimonials from known companies, but zero independent benchmarks or performance comparisons against competitors. No peer validation.

dimensions.name: Reproducibility
dimensions.rationale: Features are testable by signing up; users can verify functionality. Performance claims (startup time) could be measured independently, but Modal provides no baseline data to compare against.

dimensions.name: Conflict of Interest
dimensions.rationale: CRITICAL: This is Modal's blog announcing Modal's product. Pure self-promotion with full control over metrics, testimonials, and narrative. No third-party audit.

dimensions.name: Consensus
dimensions.rationale: Collaborative notebooks aren't novel (Colab, JupyterHub exist). Unclear if industry experts validate this as breakthrough or incremental. No independent expert consensus cited.

dimensions.name: Specificity
dimensions.rationale: Features are concrete (real-time editing, GPU configs), but performance claims like 'less than 5 seconds' are vague—less than what? Cold start vs warm? Compared to what baseline? Cherry-picked metrics without context.

dimensions.name: Track Record
dimensions.rationale: Modal ships real products, but corporate launch announcements routinely oversell incremental features as revolutionary. Track record of delivery ≠ accuracy of claims in marketing blog posts.
